title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what the benefits and drawbacks of competitive sport might be and what drives people to compete.

images.label: A
images.description: A sprinter bursting through the finish line tape at a major athletics event, arms raised in triumph while rivals trail behind

images.label: B
images.description: Two chess players facing each other across a board at a tournament, both leaning forward with intense concentration under bright spotlights

followUp.question: Do you think the pressure of competitive sport is beneficial or harmful for young athletes? Why?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what benefits recreational sport brings to people's lives and how it differs from competitive sport.

images.label: D
images.description: A group of middle-aged friends doing an early morning park run, chatting and laughing as they jog through a misty park

images.label: E
images.description: A yoga class in a bright studio, participants of various ages and body types following the instructor in a relaxed, focused atmosphere

followUp.question: Do you think it matters whether sport is competitive or recreational, as long as people are physically active?
